channel.title: Technology - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: Rocket launch costs have dropped 96% since 1960. How low can they go? - Space
item.description:
1. Rocket launch costs have dropped 96% since 1960. How low can they go?  Space
  2. Space Launch Costs Have Fallen 96% Since 1960. They Aren’t Done Yet.  SingularityHub
  3. In July 2026, Cambridge economist Alessio Terzi and colleagues published the largest launch-cost dataset ever assembled — 4,400 rockets flown since 1960 — and projected that the price of putting a kilogram into orbit will fall from $3,868 last year to $273 by  Space Daily
  4. Transport of payload into space: SpaceX as the East India Company of our time  heise online
  5. New Study Expects Space Cargo Launch Costs to Fall by 50 Percent by 2030  Mobility Engineering Technology

item.title: Antikythera mechanism: the Greek computer that predicted eclipses - Euronews.com
item.description:
1. Antikythera mechanism: the Greek computer that predicted eclipses  Euronews.com
  2. The 2,000-year-old ancient Greek machine that could predict eclipses and track the movements of the sky  The Times of India
  3. The Antikythera mechanism, recovered from a Greek shipwreck and dated to roughly 100 BC, was a hand-cranked bronze computer that tracked the heavens, of unknown origin and mechanically unmatched for more than a thousand years  Space Daily
  4. Antikythera mechanism: Greek computer that predicted eclipses 2,000 years ago  Inbox.eu
